skill.name: azure-resource-visualizer
skill.description: Analyze Azure resource groups and generate Mermaid architecture diagrams that show resources, dependencies, network paths, and key configuration details.
skill.summary: Generate Azure resource group architecture diagrams from live inventory data.
skill.author: microsoft

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, hardcoded IP, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no malicious code or prompt injection; most matches are Markdown, Mermaid syntax, examples, and Azure query documentation. The skill is publishable with a warning because it can query Azure inventory through MCP or Azure CLI and write architecture documentation locally.

medium_findings.title: Azure Inventory Access Through CLI and MCP Queries
medium_findings.description: The skill directs agents to list resource groups, query all resources, and use Azure Resource Graph or Azure CLI. This is legitimate for diagram generation, but it can expose subscription inventory, resource names, topology, and configuration details when run with broad credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly use Azure CLI or MCP queries against live Azure inventory. The behavior is read-only and aligned with the skill purpose, so the risk is exposure through authorized access rather than malicious execution.

medium_findings.title: Local Architecture Documentation May Contain Sensitive Topology
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create a Markdown architecture file from discovered Azure resources. This is expected output, but generated files can preserve internal topology, resource names, regions, and relationship details in the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation requirement is direct and intentional. The sensitivity depends on the user environment, so this is a moderate data-handling concern rather than a blocking issue.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Matches Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts occur in Markdown inline code, fenced examples, Mermaid syntax, and Azure CLI guidance. No executable script, dynamic shell construction, or unsanitized command injection path was found in these files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are Markdown instructions and examples, not runnable source files. The only meaningful command behavior is documented Azure CLI usage, which is already captured as a medium operational risk.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded IP Matches Are Private Example Addresses
low_findings.description: The hardcoded IP findings are RFC 1918 address ranges inside a sample Mermaid diagram. They are illustrative network labels, not external endpoints or callback infrastructure.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The addresses are clearly private subnet examples embedded in diagram labels. There is no code that connects to them or sends data to them.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography And Reconnaissance Matches Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts match ordinary Markdown, Mermaid labels, headings, and query examples. No hashing implementation, encryption downgrade, credential capture, host reconnaissance, or malicious scanning behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows documentation text and diagram syntax. The scanner appears to have matched tokens such as graph labels, Markdown structure, and Azure inventory language without executable security impact.

content.value_statement: Azure resource groups are hard to understand from raw inventories. This skill queries resources and creates Mermaid architecture diagrams with relationships and notes.
actual_capabilities: Lists Azure resource groups when the user has not selected one.
Queries resources in a selected Azure resource group through MCP tools or Azure CLI.
Maps relationships across network, compute, data, identity, and monitoring resources.
Generates Mermaid diagrams with grouped layers and labeled connections.
Creates Markdown architecture documentation with inventory tables and relationship notes.
Uses Azure Resource Graph patterns for cross-subscription discovery when needed.
limitations: Requires authenticated Azure access with permissions to read the target resources.
Does not create, modify, or delete Azure resources.
Diagram accuracy depends on available metadata and verified relationships.
Very large environments may need multiple diagrams by layer or resource type.

use_cases.title: Document a Resource Group
use_cases.description: Create a clear architecture document for an existing Azure resource group before a handoff or review.

use_cases.title: Map Application Dependencies
use_cases.description: Identify how apps, databases, storage, identities, and networks connect inside a deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare Change Reviews
use_cases.description: Generate a current topology diagram to support planning, migration, or governance discussions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Diagram
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one known resource group.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an architecture diagram for the Azure resource group named <resource-group>. Include the resource inventory and main relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Resource Group
prompt_templates.scenario: User does not know which resource group to inspect.
prompt_templates.prompt: List my available Azure resource groups, ask me to choose one, then generate a Mermaid architecture diagram for the selected group.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on Network and Identity
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze <resource-group> and create a diagram focused on VNets, subnets, private endpoints, managed identities, and Key Vault relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Build Layered Architecture Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced documentation for a complex environment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Azure Resource Graph where helpful to document <resource-group>. Create layered Mermaid diagrams, relationship notes, and recommendations for unclear dependencies.

output_examples.input: Generate an architecture diagram for rg-payments-prod.
output_examples.output: A Markdown architecture document with a resource inventory, grouped Mermaid diagram, relationship explanations, and notes for review.

output_examples.input: Show how my app service connects to storage, SQL, and Key Vault.
output_examples.output: A focused dependency view that labels data flow, identity use, network boundaries, and configuration links.

output_examples.input: This resource group has many services. Make it easier to review.
output_examples.output: Separate diagrams by layer, plus a concise summary of compute, data, networking, security, and monitoring components.

best_practices: Use read-only Azure credentials with the narrowest subscription and resource group scope needed.
Review generated diagrams before sharing because they may include internal names and topology.
Validate uncertain relationships with Azure metadata before treating the diagram as authoritative.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill with broad production access when a scoped reader role is enough.
Do not publish generated architecture files without removing sensitive resource names or endpoints.
Do not use the skill to change resources or replace formal security assessment tooling.

faq.question: Does this skill modify Azure resources?
faq.answer: No. The instructions are for read-only analysis and documentation generation.

faq.question: What tools does it use?
faq.answer: It can use Azure MCP tools, Azure CLI, and Azure Resource Graph query patterns.

faq.question: What output does it create?
faq.answer: It creates Markdown documentation with resource inventory, Mermaid diagrams, relationship details, and notes.

faq.question: Can it handle large environments?
faq.answer: Yes, but complex environments may need multiple diagrams separated by layer or service type.

faq.question: Is sensitive data included?
faq.answer: It may include internal resource names, topology, regions, and configuration details from Azure metadata.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Cloud engineers, solution architects, and DevOps teams who need current Azure architecture documentation.
